name: Luis Elizondo
role: Director, Advanced Aerospace Threat Identification Program (AATIP)
affiliation: Office of the Under Secretary of Defense for Intelligence, Advanced Aerospace Threat Identification Program
summary: Former Pentagon intelligence official who claims to have directed AATIP — the classified program investigating military UAP encounters. Resigned in October 2017 citing institutional indifference, simultaneously triggering the New York Times investigation that launched the modern UAP disclosure era. Co-founded To The Stars Academy and has spent seven years building the public and congressional case for UAP transparency.

name: Dr. Dan Burisch
role: Senior Microbiologist / Majestic-12 Operative
affiliation: Majestic-12, Committee of the Majority
summary: Microbiologist who claims to have worked at S-4 (Papoose Lake) under Project Aquarius, conducting biological research on extraterrestrial entities, leading multiple classified sub-projects, and serving as Majestic-12's designated disclosure officer before the organization's formal adjournment in 2005.

name: David Fravor
role: Commanding Officer, VFA-41 'Black Aces' Strike Fighter Squadron
affiliation: United States Navy, VFA-41 Black Aces, USS Nimitz Carrier Strike Group
summary: Retired U.S. Navy Commander and decorated fighter pilot who, on November 14, 2004, observed a 40-foot white tic-tac-shaped object performing impossible maneuvers off the coast of San Diego. The Nimitz encounter is the most credible documented UAP incident in modern history, corroborated by four aviators, two radar systems, and confirmed authentic by the Pentagon in 2019. Fravor testified under oath before the House Oversight Committee on July 26, 2023 — the first military aviator to do so in a modern congressional hearing.

name: Karl Nell
role: Retired Army Colonel, INSCOM; Co-Founder, Sol Foundation
affiliation: United States Army INSCOM, Army Special Forces, Raytheon Technologies, Sol Foundation
summary: Retired U.S. Army Colonel with 28 years in INSCOM and Special Forces who testified before the House Oversight Committee on July 26, 2023, providing senior military corroboration for the UAP disclosure narrative. Co-founded the Sol Foundation alongside David Grusch and Dr. Garry Nolan — the first seriously academic-affiliated UAP research organization. His most cited statement: 'Non-human intelligence exists and has been interacting with humanity.'

name: David Grusch
role: NRO Representative to the DoD UAP Task Force / NGA Senior Technical Advisor
affiliation: National Reconnaissance Office, National Geospatial Intelligence Agency, DoD UAP Task Force
summary: Former Air Force intelligence officer and NRO veteran who filed a formal Disclosure of Urgent Concern with the IC Inspector General in May 2022, alleging the U.S. government operates a multi-decade crash retrieval and reverse engineering program concealed from Congress. His complaint was deemed 'credible and urgent.' He testified under oath before the House Oversight Committee in July 2023 — the most significant public UAP congressional hearing in modern history.

name: Dr. Harold E. Puthoff
role: Director, STARGATE Remote Viewing Program; Founder, EarthTech International; TTSA Co-Founder
affiliation: NSA, SRI International, EarthTech International, To The Stars Academy
summary: Physicist with a 50-year thread through U.S. government exotic research: NSA employee (1960s), director of the CIA/DIA-funded STARGATE remote viewing program at SRI International (1972-1985), author of at least 6 of the 38 Pentagon-contracted AAWSAP Defense Intelligence Reference Documents on warp drives and traversable wormholes (2008-2010), and TTSA co-founder alongside Luis Elizondo (2017). The richest verifiable government document trail of any UAP research figure.

name: Dr. Garry Nolan
role: Professor of Pathology, Stanford School of Medicine; CIA UAP Consultant
affiliation: Stanford University School of Medicine, Sol Foundation, UAPX
summary: Tenured Stanford professor with 300+ peer-reviewed publications and 50+ U.S. patents, engaged by the CIA around 2011 to study approximately 100 individuals — pilots and intelligence officers — who reported neurological effects following UAP encounters. Found anomalous brain morphology in a significant proportion of subjects. Co-published the first peer-reviewed UAP methodology paper in a major aerospace journal (2022). Co-founded the Sol Foundation with Karl Nell and David Grusch. His institutional standing is the strongest of any public UAP figure; his claims about material recoveries and NHI presence go beyond his published evidence.

name: Robert T. Bigelow
role: Founder, NIDS / BAASS; AAWSAP Contract Holder; UAP Research Patron
affiliation: National Institute for Discovery Science (NIDS), Bigelow Aerospace, BAASS
summary: Las Vegas real estate billionaire whose private funding and government contracting activities represent the most significant private investment in classified UAP research in American history. Founded NIDS (1995) - the research organization that bridged the 1990s Las Vegas UAP community (George Knapp, Bob Lazar era) with the modern disclosure ecosystem. His company BAASS received the $22M DIA AAWSAP contract (2007-2012), producing 38 classified DIRDs through Hal Puthoff and Eric Davis. In a May 2017 CBS 60 Minutes interview, stated he is 'absolutely convinced' that ETs exist and have visited Earth.

name: Dr. Eric W. Davis
role: Senior Research Physicist, EarthTech International; AAWSAP DIRD Author
affiliation: EarthTech International, Aerospace Corporation, National Institute for Discovery Science (NIDS)
summary: Astrophysicist who co-authored at least four classified Defense Intelligence Reference Documents (DIRDs) under the AAWSAP contract, covering traversable wormholes, warp drives, and antigravity. In April 2019 he briefed the Defense Department's Aerospace Science Board and Senate Armed Services Committee in closed session. In October 2020, the New York Times quoted him stating he briefed Congress about 'off-world vehicles not made on this earth.' Best known publicly as the subject of the Wilson-Davis memo - a leaked 2002 document recording Vice Admiral Thomas R. Wilson's account of being denied access to a private aerospace contractor running a UAP crash retrieval program.

name: Christopher K. Mellon
role: Deputy Assistant Secretary of Defense for Intelligence; TTSA Senior Advisor
affiliation: Office of the Secretary of Defense, Senate Select Committee on Intelligence, To The Stars Academy
summary: Former Deputy Assistant Secretary of Defense for Intelligence and Senate Intelligence Committee staff director who held oversight responsibility for the most sensitive U.S. intelligence programs. Credited with obtaining and facilitating release of the Nimitz FLIR1, Gimbal, and GoFast videos through the December 2017 New York Times investigation. The most consequential legislative advocate for UAP transparency in Washington, having directly drafted or influenced every major UAP provision enacted since 2020 — including the UAP Task Force and AARO.

name: Bob Lazar
role: Physicist / S-4 Contractor
affiliation: S-4 Facility (Project Galileo) via EG&G
summary: Physicist who claims to have been contracted to S-4 at Papoose Lake, where he worked on reverse-engineering extraterrestrial propulsion systems. Disclosed the existence of nine alien craft and element 115 (moscovium) as a propulsion fuel source — a claim that preceded element 115's official synthesis by 15 years.

name: Nick Pope
role: UFO Desk Analyst, UK Ministry of Defence — Secretariat (Air Staff) 2a
affiliation: UK Ministry of Defence, Secretariat (Air Staff) 2a
summary: British civil servant who ran the UK Ministry of Defence's official UFO investigation desk from 1991 to 1994 — making him the closest British equivalent to Luis Elizondo. Assigned as a skeptic with Top Secret access to classified military incident reports, radar data, and witness testimony, he emerged from the role concluding that a genuine percentage of cases could not be explained by conventional means. Investigated the Cosford Incident (March 1993), a mass sighting across Shropshire and the West Midlands corroborated by trained military observers, which he assessed as the strongest British UAP case on record. His 1996 book 'Open Skies, Closed Minds' was the first firsthand account ever published by someone who held an actual government UAP investigation desk.

name: Jake Barber
role: U.S. Air Force Special Operations Veteran / UAP Retrieval Program Whistleblower
affiliation: United States Air Force (Special Operations); Unidentified Government Contractor
summary: Air Force special operations veteran who came forward publicly in January 2025 to disclose firsthand participation in a covert UAP crash retrieval program. In an exclusive interview with journalist Ross Coulthart for NewsNation, Barber presented never-before-seen video of an egg-shaped craft retrieval and was corroborated on camera by three additional military veterans. The Pentagon confirmed it was investigating his claims following broadcast — making this one of the most operationally specific UAP disclosures since David Grusch's 2023 testimony.

name: Dr. Timothy Gallaudet
role: Rear Admiral, U.S. Navy (Ret.) / Former Acting NOAA Administrator
affiliation: United States Navy, NOAA, Sol Foundation
summary: Retired U.S. Navy Rear Admiral and former Acting NOAA Administrator with a Ph.D. in Oceanography from Scripps Institution of Oceanography. One of the most scientifically credentialed figures in the modern UAP disclosure movement. Stated publicly that 'we are in contact with non-human intelligence,' testified before the House Oversight Committee in November 2024, and appeared in two Ross Coulthart NewsNation specials focused on Unidentified Submersible Objects (USOs). His combination of Naval oceanography expertise and senior government access makes him the field's foremost authority on the oceanic UAP domain.

name: Dr. Jacques F. Vallée
role: Astrophysicist, Computer Scientist; Scientific Advisor, NIDS; Author - Forbidden Science
affiliation: Northwestern University (with Hynek), SRI International, NIDS, Sol Foundation
summary: French-American astrophysicist and computer scientist who co-developed the first computerized map of Mars at the Paris Observatory, worked alongside J. Allen Hynek on the official U.S. Air Force UAP investigation (Project Blue Book), and has spent six decades as the field's most credentialed scientific voice. Uniquely documented: his Forbidden Science diary series (4 volumes, 1957-2009) records contemporaneous private conversations with government researchers, intelligence officials, and UAP witnesses. Best known for his critique of the extraterrestrial hypothesis in favor of the Control System / Interdimensional Hypothesis, and for co-authoring a peer-reviewed UAP methodology paper in a mainstream aerospace journal with Dr. Garry Nolan (2022).

name: Harry Reid
role: U.S. Senate Majority Leader; Architect of AAWSAP/AATIP Funding
affiliation: United States Senate, Senate Democratic Caucus
summary: Served as U.S. Senate Majority Leader from 2007 to 2015. In 2007, working with Senators Ted Stevens and Daniel Inouye, Reid secretly earmarked $22 million in the defense budget to create the Advanced Aerospace Weapon System Applications Program (AAWSAP) at the DIA - the first formally funded Pentagon UAP research program in decades and the direct predecessor to AATIP, the UAP Task Force, and AARO. Without Reid's political will, the modern U.S. government UAP research infrastructure does not exist. He died in December 2021 having grown increasingly vocal about his belief that the government was concealing recovered craft of non-human origin from Congress.

name: Dr. J. Allen Hynek
role: Astrophysicist; U.S. Air Force Scientific Consultant; Founder, CUFOS
affiliation: Ohio State University, Northwestern University, U.S. Air Force Projects Sign/Grudge/Blue Book, Center for UFO Studies (CUFOS)
summary: The intellectual founder of serious scientific UAP research. Recruited by the U.S. Air Force in 1948 as a skeptic to debunk UFO reports, he spent 21 years examining hundreds of military and civilian cases and emerged as the field's most credentialed scientific advocate. Invented the Close Encounter classification system (CE1/CE2/CE3) that remains the global standard. Founded CUFOS (1973), the first civilian scientific UAP organization. His trajectory from institutional debunker to scientific advocate established the template followed by every credentialed figure who has come after him.

name: George Knapp
role: Chief Investigative Reporter, KLAS-TV Las Vegas; UAP Journalist
affiliation: KLAS-TV (CBS Las Vegas), Mystery Wire, Weaponized Podcast
summary: The most consequential UAP investigative journalist in American history. His 1989 interviews with Bob Lazar launched the modern Area 51 mythology. His introduction of Senator Harry Reid to Robert Bigelow was the catalyst for AAWSAP's creation. Over 38 years he has maintained deeper source access across government, military, and intelligence communities on UAP than any other journalist. Co-authored 'Skinwalkers at the Pentagon' (2021) with DIA insider James Lacatski. Testified before Congress in September 2025. His awards include Edward R. Murrow Awards and multiple Emmys earned across non-UAP investigative work, establishing professional credibility independent of the subject.

name: Leslie Kean
role: Investigative Journalist; Co-Author, 2017 New York Times AATIP Investigation
affiliation: The New York Times (contributor), Coalition for Freedom of Information
summary: The investigative journalist most directly responsible for triggering the modern UAP disclosure era. Co-authored the December 16, 2017 New York Times article 'Glowing Auras and Black Money' with Helene Cooper and Ralph Blumenthal - the article that revealed AATIP, named Elizondo, published the Nimitz Tic Tac video, and launched the chain of events that produced congressional hearings and AARO. Her 2010 book 'UFOs: Generals, Pilots and Government Officials Go on the Record,' built entirely on named, credentialed military sources, established the methodological template a decade before the disclosure. Without her decade of groundwork and the 2017 article, the modern disclosure era does not happen when or how it did.

name: Ryan Graves
role: F/A-18 Navy Pilot; Founder, Americans for Safe Aerospace
affiliation: United States Navy (VFA-11), Americans for Safe Aerospace
summary: Retired U.S. Navy F/A-18 pilot who documented sustained near-daily UAP encounters off the East Coast in 2014-2015 following an AESA radar upgrade. Unlike single-event encounters, the VFA-11 squadron reported months of persistent UAP activity in designated training airspace. Testified under oath before the House Oversight Committee in July 2023 alongside Grusch and Fravor, and founded Americans for Safe Aerospace to provide a safe reporting channel for aviators.

name: Ross Coulthart
role: Senior Investigative Journalist, NewsNation; Author - In Plain Sight
affiliation: NewsNation, Nine Network Australia
summary: Australian investigative journalist whose UAP reporting produced several of the most significant disclosures of the 2020s. Conducted the exclusive 2023 NewsNation interview with David Grusch that triggered the modern phase of congressional UAP engagement. His January 2025 interviews with Jake Barber - including never-before-seen retrieval footage and four corroborating veterans - represent the most operationally specific disclosure since Grusch. His 2021 book 'In Plain Sight' built the documentary case from primary intelligence and military sources.

name: Dr. James T. Lacatski
role: DIA Program Manager, AAWSAP; Co-Author - Skinwalkers at the Pentagon
affiliation: Defense Intelligence Agency, Defense Warning Office
summary: The Defense Intelligence Agency officer who designed and managed AAWSAP from inside government - the first formally funded U.S. UAP research program in decades. While Reid secured the political appropriation and Bigelow held the contract, Lacatski was the government official who actually ran the program, overseeing 38 Defense Intelligence Reference Documents. He reportedly witnessed an anomalous event at Skinwalker Ranch that galvanized his commitment to the program. Co-authored 'Skinwalkers at the Pentagon' (2021) - the most detailed insider account of AAWSAP ever published.

name: Richard Dolan
role: Historian; Author - UFOs and the National Security State
affiliation: Richard Dolan Media
summary: Cold War historian who produced the most rigorous archival analysis of U.S. government UAP documentation in 'UFOs and the National Security State' (2002, 2009). Approached UAP through declassified FOIA documents, government archives, and institutional records rather than witness testimony. Coined the term 'breakaway civilization' to describe the hypothesis that a technologically superior secret civilization has emerged within the classified world. His work laid the analytical foundation that journalists and researchers drew on as historical context for understanding AATIP and the modern disclosure era.

name: Daniel Sheehan
role: Constitutional Attorney; General Counsel, Disclosure Project
affiliation: Christic Institute, Romero Institute, Disclosure Project
summary: Harvard-trained constitutional attorney who represented the New York Times in the Pentagon Papers case and served as General Counsel for the 2001 Disclosure Project National Press Club event. In 1977, claims to have viewed classified Air Force photographs of a crashed disc and recovered bodies while researching extraterrestrial intelligence for a Congressional Research Service briefing to the Carter administration. Has served as a legal advocate and institutional strategist for UAP disclosure for nearly five decades.

name: Chuck Schumer
role: U.S. Senate Majority Leader; Sponsor, UAP Disclosure Act of 2023
affiliation: United States Senate, Senate Democratic Caucus
summary: U.S. Senate Majority Leader who introduced the UAP Disclosure Act of 2023 as an amendment to the NDAA - the most significant legislative attempt to compel government UAP transparency since the 1960s. Co-authored with Senator Mike Rounds (R-SD), the original bill proposed a nine-member Presidential Review Board modeled on the JFK Records Act with authority to declassify UAP records and retrieved materials. Though significantly weakened before final passage, Schumer's sponsorship represented the most senior Senate leadership engagement with UAP as a formal legislative priority since Harry Reid's AAWSAP earmark.

name: Alex Dietrich
role: F/A-18F Pilot, VFA-41 Black Aces; Nimitz Encounter Witness
affiliation: United States Navy, VFA-41 Black Aces, USS Nimitz Carrier Strike Group
summary: Retired U.S. Navy Lieutenant Commander who was David Fravor's wingman during the November 14, 2004 Nimitz UAP encounter. Maintained altitude as the designated observer while Fravor dove toward the object - making her account the primary independent corroboration of Fravor's encounter. Kept her experience private for 17 years before going public in a May 2021 CBS 60 Minutes interview, becoming the first female military UAP witness to come forward in the modern disclosure era.

name: Kevin Day
role: Navy Radar Operator, USS Princeton; Nimitz Encounter Witness
affiliation: United States Navy, USS Princeton (CG-59), Nimitz Carrier Strike Group
summary: Retired U.S. Navy Senior Chief Operations Specialist who tracked the Nimitz UAP objects on USS Princeton's SPY-1B radar for approximately two weeks before the November 14, 2004 visual encounter. Day's radar contacts showed objects descending from above 80,000 feet to sea level in seconds. He coordinated the intercept that directed Fravor toward the object, making him the radar-side primary witness to the Nimitz incident. Has spoken publicly about the psychological impact of the experience.

name: Dr. John E. Mack
role: Professor of Psychiatry, Harvard Medical School; Founder of PEER
affiliation: Harvard Medical School, Program for Extraordinary Experience Research (PEER)
summary: Pulitzer Prize-winning Harvard psychiatrist who founded the Program for Extraordinary Experience Research (PEER) in 1993 and became the most academically credentialed researcher to seriously investigate UAP contact experiences. Investigated the 1994 Ariel School incident in Zimbabwe. Died in London in 2004.

name: Jesse Michels
role: Independent Journalist and Filmmaker; Founder, American Alchemy
affiliation: American Alchemy (YouTube)
summary: Founder and host of American Alchemy, a YouTube channel with 567K+ subscribers covering UAP disclosure, fringe science, and insider testimony. Conducted one of David Grusch's first major media appearances before his congressional testimony in 2023. Has interviewed nearly every significant figure in modern UAP disclosure across 350+ episodes, making American Alchemy one of the most consequential independent journalism outlets in the field.

name: Dr. Harald Malmgren
role: Presidential Adviser (Kennedy, Johnson, Nixon, Ford); Deputy Special Trade Representative
affiliation: National Security Council, Office of the U.S. Trade Representative
summary: One of the longest-serving senior White House advisers of the Cold War era, Malmgren served four consecutive presidential administrations with NSC situation room access and Q clearance. In a 2025 interview with Jesse Michels he disclosed firsthand handling of anomalous material recovered from the Bluegill Triple Prime nuclear test (1962) in the Marshall Islands, and a private briefing from CIA Deputy Director Richard Bissell on a 1933 Italian UFO crash - despite which he was denied further access to recovered material information. His government career is among the most senior of any figure to have made firsthand UAP material claims on record.

name: Stanton T. Friedman
role: Nuclear Physicist; UFO Researcher
affiliation: Independent; Center for UFO Studies (CUFOS)
summary: Nuclear physicist who spent 52 years as the world's foremost civilian Roswell researcher. Located and first interviewed Jesse Marcel Sr. in 1978, resurrecting the Roswell case from decades of obscurity. Coined 'Cosmic Watergate' to describe systematic government UAP suppression. Gave over 700 lectures on college campuses and applied rigorous document analysis to the MJ-12 papers.

name: Dr. Avi Loeb
role: Harvard Astrophysicist; Director, Galileo Project
affiliation: Harvard University; Galileo Project
summary: Harvard's former astronomy chair who founded the Galileo Project in 2021 - the first systematic, peer-reviewed, fully transparent scientific program to search for extraterrestrial technological artifacts including UAP. His willingness to apply mainstream academic rigor to the UAP question, combined with his institutional standing, has given the field unprecedented credibility in academic circles. Has published over 1,000 peer-reviewed papers and testified before Congress in 2024.

name: Maj. Donald E. Keyhoe
role: Director, NICAP; Marine Corps Major (Ret.)
affiliation: National Investigations Committee on Aerial Phenomena (NICAP)
summary: Marine Corps Major who co-founded and directed NICAP from 1957 to 1969, making it the largest and most credible civilian UFO organization in American history with 14,000 members. His 1950 book 'The Flying Saucers Are Real' sold half a million copies and established government accountability as the central framework for UAP disclosure advocacy - 60 years before the modern disclosure movement.

name: Col. Philip J. Corso
role: U.S. Army Colonel, Pentagon Foreign Technology Division
affiliation: U.S. Army, Pentagon Foreign Technology Division
summary: Retired U.S. Army Colonel who served on Eisenhower's NSC before assignment to the Pentagon's Army R&D division under Lt. General Arthur Trudeau. In his 1997 memoir The Day After Roswell, Corso claimed he managed a program to seed Roswell crash debris to private defense contractors, allegedly producing fiber optics, integrated circuits, night vision, and Kevlar. His account is the most detailed military insider claim about systematic Roswell artifact reverse engineering ever published.

name: Dr. Steven M. Greer
role: Founder, CSETI and The Disclosure Project
affiliation: CSETI, The Disclosure Project
summary: Emergency physician turned full-time UAP disclosure advocate who organized the landmark May 9, 2001 National Press Club briefing with 20+ military and government witnesses. Founded CSETI (1990) and The Disclosure Project (1993). One of the most prominent and polarizing figures in UAP disclosure, praised for aggregating witness testimony and criticized for CE-5 contact claims and fundraising practices.

name: Jay Stratton
role: Director, DoD UAP Task Force (2020-2021); AARO Director (2022, acting)
affiliation: Office of Naval Intelligence, DoD UAP Task Force, AARO
summary: Career Defense Intelligence officer who served as Director of the DoD UAP Task Force (2020-2021) and briefly as AARO's first director in 2022. Worked closely with Luis Elizondo during the AATIP era and is considered a key institutional continuity figure bridging AATIP, the UAPTF, and AARO. David Grusch has described Stratton as the government official who directed him to investigate legacy UAP crash retrieval programs.

name: Robert L. Hastings
role: Researcher and Author - UFOs and Nukes
affiliation: Independent researcher
summary: Civilian researcher who has spent over four decades documenting UAP incidents at nuclear weapons facilities, compiling testimony from over 160 U.S. military veterans. His 2008 book UFOs and Nukes is the definitive compilation of military testimony connecting UAP to nuclear sites. In 2010 he organized a National Press Club event at which seven USAF veterans described firsthand UAP encounters at missile bases, including ICBM shutdowns.

name: Dr. Sean M. Kirkpatrick
role: Director, All-domain Anomaly Resolution Office (AARO), 2022-2023
affiliation: All-domain Anomaly Resolution Office, Defense Intelligence Agency
summary: PhD physicist who served as the first official director of AARO from November 2022 until his resignation in December 2023. Testified before the Senate in April 2023 and co-authored a paper with Avi Loeb on UAP signatures. Resigned citing lack of institutional support and congressional interference. His departure and subsequent public criticism marked growing controversy over AARO effectiveness.

name: Dylan Borland
role: USAF GEOINT Specialist, Federal UAP Whistleblower
affiliation: U.S. Air Force / Defense Contractor
summary: Former USAF GEOINT specialist who reported a close-range triangle UAP sighting at Langley AFB in 2012. Went through official whistleblower channels (ICIG, AARO) before testifying under oath before the House Oversight Committee in September 2025.

name: Matthew Brown
role: Policy/Technical Advisor, OSD and State Dept.; IMMACULATE CONSTELLATION Whistleblower
affiliation: Office of the Secretary of Defense / U.S. Department of State
summary: Former DoD and State Department national security professional who submitted an 11-page Congressional report in November 2024 describing IMMACULATE CONSTELLATION, an alleged USAP collecting imagery intelligence on UAP under NSC authority. Went public in April 2025 on the WEAPONIZED podcast alongside Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp.

name: Dr. Christopher "Kit" Green
role: CIA Senior Analyst (Weird Desk); AAWSAP DIRD Author; Forensic Neurologist
affiliation: CIA / General Motors / Wayne State University / BAASS research network
summary: Former CIA Senior Division Analyst who maintained custodianship of the agency's internal UAP files (the 'Weird Desk') for over a decade and briefed two CIA directors on UAP phenomena. A board-certified forensic neurologist, he authored a 2009 AAWSAP Defense Intelligence Reference Document on physiological injuries from UAP proximity, then co-investigated neurological anomalies in UAP-affected government personnel with Dr. Garry Nolan.

name: Dr. Colm A. Kelleher
role: Deputy Administrator, NIDS; Senior Researcher, BAASS/AAWSAP
affiliation: National Institute for Discovery Science / Bigelow Aerospace Advanced Space Studies
summary: Biochemist who led the seven-year NIDS scientific investigation of Skinwalker Ranch under Robert Bigelow, then served as a senior researcher at BAASS during the DIA's $22M AAWSAP program. Co-authored 'Hunt for the Skinwalker' (2005) with George Knapp and 'Skinwalkers at the Pentagon' (2021) with James Lacatski and Knapp - two of the most important primary source accounts of private and government UAP research.

name: Annie Jacobsen
role: National Security Journalist; Author - Area 51, Phenomena, UFO
affiliation: Los Angeles Times / Little, Brown and Company
summary: Investigative journalist whose three UAP-adjacent books represent the most sourced mainstream journalism on U.S. government anomalous research programs. 'Area 51' (2011) drew on 74 firsthand sources. 'Phenomena' (2017) documented the CIA/DIA Star Gate remote viewing program through primary sources including Hal Puthoff. 'UFO' (2023) is the most sourced journalistic account of UAP program infrastructure, independently corroborating Grusch-era whistleblower disclosures from a completely different government source network.

name: Jim Semivan
role: CIA Clandestine Services Officer (ret.); SOL Foundation Co-founder
affiliation: Central Intelligence Agency / SOL Foundation
summary: Former CIA Clandestine Services officer with 25 years of operational experience who has publicly disclosed a personal UAP/NHI encounter he and his wife experienced in their home. Co-founded the SOL Foundation with Dr. Garry Nolan to bridge intelligence community awareness of the phenomenon with rigorous academic scientific inquiry. A founding advisory board member of To The Stars Academy, Semivan represents a rare intersection of career intelligence operations experience and public UAP disclosure advocacy.

name: Senator Kirsten Gillibrand
role: U.S. Senator (D-NY); UAP Disclosure Act Co-sponsor
affiliation: U.S. Senate / Senate Armed Services Committee
summary: The most consequential legislative figure in UAP transparency of the 2021-2024 period. Established the Senate Armed Services Subcommittee on Emerging Threats and Capabilities with an explicit UAP oversight mandate, co-sponsored the UAP Disclosure Act with Chuck Schumer, and chaired the first dedicated Senate UAP hearing in June 2023. Her sustained multi-year campaign to compel executive branch transparency produced the most durable legislative infrastructure for UAP accountability in modern American history.

name: Rep. Tim Burchett
role: U.S. Representative (R-TN); House Oversight UAP Hearing Co-chair
affiliation: U.S. House of Representatives
summary: U.S. Representative from Tennessee and co-chair of the House UAP Caucus who co-led the landmark July 2023 House Oversight Committee hearing where David Grusch, Ryan Graves, and David Fravor testified under oath. One of the most outspoken congressional voices on UAP disclosure, Burchett has sustained a multi-year campaign for whistleblower protections and full government transparency on non-human intelligence.

name: John Burroughs
role: USAF Staff Sergeant (ret.); Rendlesham Forest Primary Witness
affiliation: U.S. Air Force / 81st Security Police Squadron
summary: Former U.S. Air Force Staff Sergeant and primary ground witness to the Rendlesham Forest incident (December 1980), one of the most documented UAP cases in history, in which USAF personnel at RAF Bentwaters/Woodbridge encountered an unidentified craft at close range over three consecutive nights. Burroughs later fought a years-long legal battle with the VA over cardiac health issues he attributes to radiation exposure, winning a 2015 landmark settlement that required Senate Armed Services Committee intervention to compel review of his classified medical records - an unprecedented outcome in any UAP-related health case.

name: Capt. Edward J. Ruppelt
role: USAF Captain; Project Blue Book Chief (1951-1953)
affiliation: U.S. Air Force / Project Blue Book
summary: U.S. Air Force Captain who served as chief of Project Blue Book during its most scientifically rigorous period, coined the term 'Unidentified Flying Object,' and after leaving the Air Force published the most candid insider account of official UAP investigation ever released. His 1956 book documented cases the Air Force could not explain and criticized the Robertson Panel's debunking mandate - a record that became foundational to serious UAP research in the decades that followed.

name: Dr. James E. McDonald
role: Senior Physicist, Univ. of Arizona; UAP Scientific Investigator
affiliation: University of Arizona / Institute of Atmospheric Physics
summary: Senior atmospheric physicist at the University of Arizona who became the most credentialed scientist to seriously investigate UAP cases during the Cold War era. His 1969 critique 'Science in Default' demonstrated that the Condon Report had systematically ignored the strongest cases in its own sample, and his 1968 congressional testimony was among the first by a credentialed scientist arguing UAP represented a genuine unsolved scientific problem.

name: Prof. Diana Walsh Pasulka
role: Professor of Religious Studies, UNCW; American Cosmic author
affiliation: University of North Carolina Wilmington
summary: Pasulka is the foremost academic researcher at the intersection of UAP phenomena and religious studies, gaining unprecedented access to anonymous government and technology industry insiders who allowed her to document their work firsthand. Her Oxford University Press book American Cosmic (2019) and follow-up Encounters (2023) argue that UAP experiences are reshaping belief systems in ways that parallel historical religious transformations.

name: Thomas Townsend Brown
role: U.S. Navy Scientist; Biefeld-Brown Effect discoverer
affiliation: U.S. Navy / Townsend Brown Foundation
summary: Mid-century American physicist whose research into the relationship between electromagnetism and gravity produced the Biefeld-Brown effect and drew him into classified government aerospace programs. His 1953 Project Winterhaven proposal for a disc-shaped electrogravitic aircraft and alleged vacuum experiments confirming thrust independent of ionic wind place him at the center of ongoing debates about whether anti-gravity principles were absorbed into black aerospace programs. Cited by modern UAP researchers as historical context for reverse-engineering claims.

name: Adm. Thomas R. Wilson
role: Director, Defense Intelligence Agency (1999-2002)
affiliation: Defense Intelligence Agency
summary: Retired Vice Admiral and former DIA Director who is the central figure in the Wilson-Davis Memo - a leaked document describing his alleged confirmation that a private contractor was reverse-engineering non-human technology under a classified SAP, and that he had been denied access despite his rank.

name: Lt. Col. Charles Halt
role: Deputy Base Commander, RAF Bentwaters/Woodbridge; Senior Rendlesham Witness
affiliation: U.S. Air Force
summary: Retired U.S. Air Force Lieutenant Colonel and Deputy Base Commander at RAF Bentwaters who was the senior military witness to the Rendlesham Forest incident (December 1980). Authored the official 'Halt Memo' to the UK Ministry of Defence and recorded a real-time audio log during the encounter. Has stated under oath that what he witnessed was non-human technology and that a government cover-up followed.

name: Sen. Marco Rubio
role: U.S. Senator (FL); Chairman, Senate Intelligence Committee (2020-2021)
affiliation: U.S. Senate
summary: U.S. Senator from Florida and former Chairman of the Senate Intelligence Committee who authored the 2020 UAP Task Force legislative mandate - the first official government UAP reporting body since Project Blue Book. Has consistently framed UAP as a national security concern and pushed for government transparency.

name: Rep. Mike Gallagher
role: U.S. Representative (WI-R); House Armed Services & Intelligence Committees
affiliation: U.S. House of Representatives
summary: Former U.S. Representative and Marine Corps intelligence officer who co-chaired the landmark July 2023 House Oversight UAP hearing featuring David Grusch, Ryan Graves, and David Fravor, and co-authored the UAP Disclosure Act of 2023 with Sen. Chuck Schumer. His analytical, non-sensationalist approach gave the 2023 UAP hearings significant institutional credibility.

name: Dr. Peter Sturrock
role: Professor Emeritus of Applied Physics, Stanford University; Founder, Society for Scientific Exploration
affiliation: Stanford University
summary: Stanford Professor Emeritus of Applied Physics who organized the 1997 Sturrock Panel - a rigorous scientific review of physical UAP evidence by nine independent scientists - and founded the Society for Scientific Exploration, providing the first mainstream academic infrastructure for anomalous phenomena research. His peer-reviewed work concluded that physical UAP evidence warranted serious scientific investigation.

name: Gen. Nathan Twining
role: Chairman, Joint Chiefs of Staff (1957-1960); Chief of Staff, USAF (1953-1957)
affiliation: U.S. Air Force
summary: Four-star General and Chairman of the Joint Chiefs who authored the pivotal 1947 'Twining Memo' - an authenticated government document concluding that flying saucers are 'real and not visionary or fictitious' and recommending the classified study group that became Projects Sign, Grudge, and Blue Book. The Twining Memo is one of the most significant official UAP documents in existence.

name: Maj. Jesse Marcel Sr.
role: Base Intelligence Officer, 509th Bomb Group, Roswell Army Air Field (1947)
affiliation: U.S. Army Air Forces
summary: U.S. Army Air Forces intelligence officer who was the primary military figure to recover and examine the Roswell crash debris in July 1947. In a landmark 1978 interview with Stanton Friedman, Marcel became the first credible military whistleblower to publicly state that the weather balloon explanation was false and that the recovered material had anomalous properties unlike anything he had encountered.

name: James Fox
role: Documentary Filmmaker; Producer, 'The Phenomenon' (2020)
affiliation: Independent Filmmaker
summary: Documentary filmmaker and investigative journalist who produced 'The Phenomenon' (2020) - widely considered the most credible UAP documentary ever made, featuring on-record statements from multiple heads of state, senior military officials, and scientists including Jacques Vallee. His films contributed to the 2020-2021 disclosure wave and are distinguished by their insistence on institutional, on-the-record sources.

name: Gov. Fife Symington
role: Governor of Arizona (1991-1997); U.S. Air Force Captain
affiliation: State of Arizona
summary: Former Arizona Governor and Air Force veteran who initially mocked the March 1997 Phoenix Lights event with a staged alien costume press conference, then publicly recanted in 2007 - stating he personally witnessed a massive structured craft that night and that it was unlike any known aircraft. His reversal as a credentialed military officer and former governor is one of the most significant official UAP admissions on record.

name: Michael Herrera
role: U.S. Marine Corps Veteran; UAP Whistleblower
affiliation: U.S. Marine Corps (former)
summary: U.S. Marine Corps veteran who testified at the July 2023 House Oversight UAP hearing and filed a sworn ICIG complaint claiming his six-man patrol encountered a massive octagonal craft in Sumatra in 2009, being loaded by personnel in unmarked uniforms who subsequently threatened his patrol to silence. His account implies direct human interaction with non-human technology through a classified Special Access Program.

name: Sen. Barry Goldwater
role: U.S. Senator, Arizona; Chairman, Senate Armed Services Committee
affiliation: U.S. Senate; U.S. Air Force Reserve
summary: U.S. Senator from Arizona for over three decades, USAF Major General, and Senate Armed Services Committee Chairman who repeatedly and publicly stated that the U.S. government held classified UAP evidence and denied him personal access. His confrontation with General Curtis LeMay over the 'Blue Room' at Wright-Patterson AFB - documented in a 1975 letter now held by the National Archives - remains one of the most significant political admissions in UAP disclosure history.

name: Capt. Robert Salas
role: USAF Minuteman ICBM Launch Control Officer, Malmstrom AFB
affiliation: U.S. Air Force, Strategic Air Command
summary: Retired USAF Captain and primary living witness to the March 16, 1967 Echo Flight incident at Malmstrom AFB, in which all ten nuclear-armed Minuteman ICBMs went offline simultaneously while surface security personnel reported a UAP hovering over the front gate. The missile shutdowns are documented in declassified USAF records. Salas co-organized the 2010 National Press Club 'UFOs and Nuclear Weapons' press conference alongside six other former USAF officers, presenting signed affidavits before national media.

name: Dr. Edgar Mitchell
role: NASA Apollo 14 Lunar Module Pilot; Founder, Institute of Noetic Sciences
affiliation: NASA; U.S. Navy; Institute of Noetic Sciences
summary: Apollo 14 astronaut and the sixth human to walk on the Moon, holding an MIT doctorate in Aeronautics and Astronautics. Mitchell publicly maintained across four decades that extraterrestrial visitation was real and that the government had suppressed evidence of it. In 1973 he founded the Institute of Noetic Sciences (IONS), a credentialed consciousness research organization still active today. His 2015 WikiLeaks-published emails to presidential campaign chairman John Podesta, requesting urgent meetings on ETI disclosure and zero point energy, are a documented primary source connecting his convictions to the highest levels of American politics.

name: Tom DeLonge
role: Founder, To The Stars Academy of Arts & Science (TTSA)
affiliation: To The Stars Academy; To The Stars Inc.
summary: Founder of To The Stars Academy and the private citizen most responsible for the modern UAP disclosure era. Despite no institutional credentials, DeLonge assembled an unprecedented civilian team of former senior government officials and coordinated the December 2017 New York Times disclosure that forced the Pentagon's first public acknowledgment of UAP program funding. His pre-TTSA WikiLeaks emails to John Podesta document verified meetings with active USAF generals and Lockheed Skunk Works executives.

name: Col. John Alexander
role: U.S. Army Special Forces Colonel; Chief of Human Technology, INSCOM; Director, Non-Lethal Weapons, Los Alamos
affiliation: U.S. Army; INSCOM; Los Alamos National Laboratory; NIDS
summary: Retired U.S. Army Special Forces Colonel who convened the Advanced Theoretical Physics Conference (ATP, 1985-1988) - the most substantive classified interagency UAP investigation between Project Blue Book and AATIP. Held in a SCIF at BDM Corporation with TS-cleared participants from Army, Navy, Air Force, CIA, NSA, and DIA, the ATP's finding that no coordinated UAP program existed is itself a historically significant data point. Post-retirement, he directed non-lethal weapons research at Los Alamos and participated in Bigelow's NIDS Skinwalker Ranch investigations alongside Vallee and Kelleher.

name: John Callahan
role: Division Chief, Accidents and Investigations Branch, Federal Aviation Administration
affiliation: Federal Aviation Administration (FAA)
summary: Former FAA Division Chief who compiled and briefed the complete evidentiary record for the November 1986 JAL Flight 1628 incident - one of the most corroborated aviation UAP cases in history. A Japan Air Lines Boeing 747 crew reported being followed for 50 minutes over Alaska by objects including one described as twice the size of an aircraft carrier, confirmed on FAA radar at multiple facilities. Callahan testified that CIA officers subsequently confiscated all materials and instructed attendees that 'this meeting never happened,' while claiming he retained personal copies.

name: Richard Doty
role: Special Agent, AFOSI, Kirtland AFB; Primary operative in the Bennewitz disinformation operation
affiliation: U.S. Air Force Office of Special Investigations (AFOSI)
summary: Retired AFOSI special agent and the most consequential documented government disinformation operative in UAP research history. Doty managed a deliberate psychological operation against defense contractor Paul Bennewitz (1980-1988), feeding fabricated UAP evidence that ultimately contributed to Bennewitz's mental breakdown. He also showed journalist Linda Moulton Howe a fabricated classified briefing document at Kirtland AFB in 1983. He has acknowledged these activities post-retirement. His significance is not as a reliable source but as documented proof that deliberate U.S. intelligence disinformation targeting UAP researchers is a historical fact.

name: Linda Moulton Howe
role: Investigative journalist; documentary filmmaker; founder, Earthfiles.com
affiliation: KMGH-TV Denver (former); Earthfiles.com
summary: Stanford-credentialed documentary filmmaker and investigative journalist who pioneered the forensic documentation of cattle mutilations with her 1980 Regional Emmy-winning documentary 'A Strange Harvest.' A documented target of the AFOSI disinformation campaign managed by Richard Doty, who showed her a fabricated classified briefing document at Kirtland AFB in 1983. Founder of Earthfiles.com (1999), a 25-year platform of military and government source testimony on UAP and anomalous phenomena. Her career spans genuinely rigorous early journalism through increasingly controversial later claims.

name: Jeremy Corbell
role: Documentary filmmaker; co-host, Weaponized podcast
affiliation: Extraordinary Beliefs; Weaponized podcast
summary: Documentary filmmaker who forced multiple official Pentagon acknowledgments of UAP footage through strategic media releases. The USS Russell Pyramid footage, USS Omaha Metallic Orb, and Mosul Orb were all confirmed authentic by the Pentagon following his releases. Director of the definitive Bob Lazar documentary (Netflix, 2019). Co-hosts 'Weaponized' with George Knapp. His written testimony was entered into the Congressional Record during the August 2023 House UAP hearings.

name: Whitley Strieber
role: Author - Communion (1987); host, Dreamland podcast
affiliation: Unknown Country; Dreamland
summary: Author of 'Communion' (1987), the most widely read first-person account of a contact experience ever published, which became an international bestseller and gave millions of experiencers language and cultural permission to acknowledge their own encounters. Founder of the Dreamland platform hosting UAP researchers including Vallee, Puthoff, Pasulka, and Semivan. His personal archive is held at Rice University's Woodson Research Center.

name: Kenneth Arnold
role: Private pilot; first widely-reported modern UAP witness (1947)
affiliation: Great Western Fire Control Supply; Idaho Search and Rescue
summary: Experienced Idaho businessman and private pilot whose June 24, 1947 sighting of nine high-speed objects near Mt. Rainier directly triggered the formation of Project Sign - the first official USAF UFO investigation. The term 'flying saucer' emerged from press misquotation of his description. Army Air Force investigators found him credible. His account remains internally consistent across 37 years and his sighting remains unexplained in the official record.

name: Rear Adm. Roscoe H. Hillenkoetter
role: First CIA Director (1947-1950); NICAP Board of Governors
affiliation: Central Intelligence Agency; U.S. Navy; NICAP
summary: The first Director of the Central Intelligence Agency (1947-1950), serving during the formation of Project Sign and the earliest official UAP investigations. After retirement, joined NICAP's Board of Governors alongside Donald Keyhoe, becoming the highest-credentialed official to publicly associate with civilian UAP advocacy. His 1960 New York Times-reported congressional statement asserting that Air Force officers were 'soberly concerned' about UAPs remains the most significant public disclosure by any former senior official prior to the 2017 era.

name: Dr. Bruce Maccabee
role: Optical Physicist, Naval Surface Warfare Center; UAP Photo Analyst
affiliation: Naval Surface Warfare Center; Fund for UFO Research; MUFON
summary: Navy optical physicist who conducted a parallel 36-year career analyzing UAP photographic and film evidence while employed at the Naval Surface Warfare Center. Applied professional optical instrumentation and photogrammetric methods to high-profile cases including the McMinnville photos, New Zealand Kaikoura film, and Gulf Breeze series. Co-founded the Fund for UFO Research in 1979 and remains one of the few credentialed government scientists to have dedicated a career-length effort to UAP evidence review.

name: Col. Jesse Marcel Jr.
role: Otolaryngologist; Colonel, Montana Army National Guard; Roswell Witness
affiliation: Montana Army National Guard; private medical practice, Helena MT
summary: Physician and Montana Army National Guard Colonel who, as an 11-year-old in July 1947, handled debris his father Jesse Marcel Sr. brought home from the Foster Ranch crash site before reporting to RAAF base. Maintained a consistent firsthand account of anomalous material properties across 35 years of public testimony. Co-authored 'The Roswell Legacy' (2009) and gave congressional-style testimony at the Citizen Hearing on Disclosure weeks before his death in August 2013.

name: Charles J. McCullough III
role: Inspector General of the Intelligence Community (2011-2017); UAP Whistleblower Attorney
affiliation: Office of the Director of National Intelligence; Compass Rose Legal Group
summary: Former Inspector General of the Intelligence Community (2011-2017) and legal representative for UAP whistleblower David Grusch. As the first Senate-confirmed IC IG, McCullough oversaw independent watchdog functions across all 17 U.S. intelligence agencies. His post-government validation of Grusch's whistleblower complaint - including public statements that Grusch 'is not alone' - carries unique institutional weight given his direct prior role over the office that received the complaint.

name: Wilbert B. Smith
role: Superintendent of Radio Regulations Engineering, Canadian Dept. of Transport; Director, Project Magnet
affiliation: Canadian Department of Transport; Project Magnet
summary: Senior Canadian government radio engineer who authored the classified 1950 Smith Memorandum - documenting that U.S. officials privately told him flying saucers were classified above the H-bomb and that Vannevar Bush led a group analyzing recovered hardware. Founded and directed Project Magnet (1950-1954), Canada's official government UAP investigation. His memo was declassified in 1979 and partially corroborated in 1983 when Dr. Robert Sarbacher confirmed the substance of their 1950 conversation in writing.

name: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna
role: U.S. Representative (R-FL-13); Chair, Task Force on the Declassification of Federal Secrets
affiliation: U.S. House of Representatives; House UAP Caucus; Task Force on the Declassification of Federal Secrets
summary: U.S. Representative and Air Force veteran who co-organized the landmark July 2023 House Oversight UAP hearing and chairs the House Task Force on the Declassification of Federal Secrets. Holds the most institutionally powerful House position dedicated to UAP declassification as of 2025, with formal subpoena authority. Has personally accessed classified SCIF briefings and states she viewed photographs of craft not made by mankind.

name: Dr. Lynne Kitei
role: Physician; Phoenix Lights Witness and Primary Researcher
affiliation: Private medical practice, Phoenix, AZ
summary: Phoenix physician who began privately documenting unusual aerial phenomena from her home in 1995 - two years before the mass March 13, 1997 Phoenix Lights event. Her multi-year pre-event observation record distinguishes her from other Phoenix Lights witnesses. She concealed her identity for years to protect her medical reputation before going public with 'The Phoenix Lights' (2000) and the follow-up documentary (2008), becoming the primary investigative voice for the most widely witnessed UAP event in modern American history.

name: Maj. Gen. Neil McCasland
role: Commander, AFRL Wright-Patterson AFB; Director of Special Programs, OSD; SAPOC Executive Secretary
affiliation: U.S. Air Force; Air Force Research Laboratory; Office of the Under Secretary of Defense
summary: Retired U.S. Air Force Major General and MIT-trained aerospace engineer who commanded the Air Force Research Laboratory at Wright-Patterson AFB (2011-2013) and served as Executive Secretary of the Special Access Program Oversight Committee (SAPOC) - giving him formal oversight authority over all U.S. Special Access Programs. Named in Tom DeLonge's January 2016 WikiLeaks email to John Podesta as a primary military adviser who helped assemble the disclosure advisory team that became To The Stars Academy. Disappeared from his Albuquerque home on February 27, 2026; the FBI joined the search and Rep. Tim Burchett stated publicly that McCasland 'has a lot to say' about UAP.

name: Travis Walton
role: CE4 Experiencer; Author - Fire in the Sky (1993)
affiliation: Independent; Disclosure Project
summary: Central figure in one of the most thoroughly witnessed CE4 cases on record. On November 5, 1975, Walton vanished in full view of six coworkers after approaching a hovering disc-shaped craft in the Apache-Sitgreaves National Forest near Snowflake, Arizona. He reappeared five days later with fragmented memories of being aboard a craft. Five of his six coworkers passed Arizona DPS polygraph examinations corroborating their account of the initial disappearance. His 1993 memoir Fire in the Sky and the Paramount film adaptation brought the case to mainstream awareness.

name: Budd Hopkins
role: CE4 Researcher; Author; Founder - Intruders Foundation
affiliation: Intruders Foundation
summary: Abstract expressionist painter who became the foundational figure in CE4/abduction research. Hopkins coined the term 'missing time,' pioneered hypnotic regression as an investigative methodology, and documented thousands of cases over three decades. His 1981 book 'Missing Time' established the research framework that Dr. John Mack later validated through Harvard. He founded the Intruders Foundation in 1989 and his 1996 work 'Witnessed' remains the most detailed single CE4 case study in the published literature.

name: Dr. Roger K. Leir
role: Podiatric Surgeon; Alien Implant Researcher; Founder - A&S Research Inc.
affiliation: A&S Research Inc.; National Institute for Discovery Science (affiliated)
summary: Podiatric surgeon who performed 17 documented surgical procedures to remove objects from alleged CE4 abductees between 1995 and 2014. Specimens submitted to independent labs including Los Alamos National Laboratory and UC San Diego produced anomalous findings including unusual isotopic ratios and absence of inflammatory response. His 'The Aliens and the Scalpel' (1998, 2005) remains the primary reference work on alleged alien implants in the research literature.

name: Betty and Barney Hill
role: CE4 Experiencers; Subjects of the First Widely Publicized U.S. Abduction Case
affiliation: NICAP (cooperating witnesses)
summary: Interracial couple from Portsmouth, New Hampshire, whose September 19-20, 1961 experience on U.S. Route 3 became the first widely publicized CE4 case in the United States. Under separate hypnotic regression with Boston psychiatrist Dr. Benjamin Simon, both independently described consistent accounts of being taken aboard a craft. Betty drew a star map later identified by astronomer Marjorie Fish as a possible Zeta Reticuli vantage point. The case was documented in John Fuller's 1966 bestseller 'The Interrupted Journey' and remains the foundational reference for the abduction research tradition.

name: John Lear
role: CIA Contract Pilot; Early UAP Disclosure Advocate; Author of 'The John Lear Statement' (1987)
affiliation: CIA (former contract); Disclosure Project
summary: Son of aviation pioneer William P. Lear and one of the most accomplished pilots in American aviation history, holding FAA certifications in over 100 aircraft types and setting 17 world speed records. From 1987 onward he became a central early figure in the UAP disclosure community through 'The John Lear Statement' - one of the first internet-distributed UAP disclosure documents - and through his direct role in facilitating Bob Lazar's emergence as a public figure via introduction to journalist George Knapp at KLAS-TV Las Vegas in 1989.

name: Rep. Eric Burlison
role: U.S. Representative (R-MO-7); Founding Member, House UAP Caucus
affiliation: U.S. House of Representatives
summary: Republican congressman from Missouri and founding member of the House UAP Caucus who serves on the House Oversight Committee. Notably the most skeptical member of the UAP Caucus on extraterrestrial explanations, yet among the most active legislators pursuing oversight - personally hiring David Grusch as a Special Advisor, attending SCIF briefings, introducing the UAP Disclosure Act of 2025, and publicly releasing new UAP footage at a 2025 Task Force hearing.

name: Ingo Swann
role: Remote Viewing Pioneer; Primary Subject, CIA SCANATE / STAR GATE
affiliation: Stanford Research Institute (SRI International) / CIA
summary: American psychic and author who served as the central research subject in the U.S. government's classified remote viewing programs from 1972 through 1995. Working with Hal Puthoff and Russell Targ at SRI, Swann co-developed the Coordinate Remote Viewing (CRV) protocol used across SCANATE, GRILL FLAME, and STAR GATE. His 1973 description of ring-like structures around Jupiter - documented before Voyager 1's 1979 confirmation - remains the most debated data point in the remote viewing literature. His 1998 book 'Penetration' described a secret government UAP-monitoring agency and an alleged ET base on the far side of the Moon.

name: Joseph McMoneagle
role: Remote Viewer #001, U.S. Army STAR GATE; Retired Chief Warrant Officer
affiliation: U.S. Army Intelligence / STAR GATE Program
summary: Retired U.S. Army Chief Warrant Officer and Remote Viewer #001 in the classified STAR GATE program. The only person ever to receive a formal military decoration — the Legion of Merit — explicitly crediting remote viewing with producing 'critical intelligence unavailable from any other source' for the Joint Chiefs of Staff, DIA, NSA, CIA, and Secret Service. His claimed operational results include the pre-discovery description of the Soviet Typhoon-class submarine and contributing intelligence during the 1981 kidnapping of General James Dozier. Author of 'The Stargate Chronicles' (2002).

name: Robert Monroe
role: Founder, The Monroe Institute; Inventor, Hemi-Sync Technology
affiliation: The Monroe Institute
summary: American radio broadcasting executive who began experiencing involuntary out-of-body episodes in 1958 and spent the rest of his life systematically documenting them. His 1971 book 'Journeys Out of the Body' coined the modern term OBE and his Hemi-Sync binaural beat technology drew formal CIA and Army interest. The 1983 declassified 'Analysis and Assessment of Gateway Process' is a CIA document evaluating his methods for intelligence applications and concluding they are plausible. His Institute became the training ground for government remote viewers including Joe McMoneagle, and his later books describe contact with non-human intelligent entities he called INSPEC.

name: Dr. Beatriz Villarroel
role: Astrophysicist, VASCO Principal Investigator
affiliation: Nordita / Instituto de Astrofísica de Canarias
summary: Swedish-Spanish astrophysicist whose VASCO project identified hundreds of anomalous vanishing objects in pre-satellite photographic plates. Her peer-reviewed studies in MNRAS, Scientific Reports, and PASP document transient flashes correlating with nuclear tests and the 1952 Washington D.C. UFO flap, representing some of the most rigorously published astronomical data in UAP-adjacent science.

name: Dr. Kevin Knuth
role: Professor of Physics, UAPx Lead Scientist
affiliation: University at Albany (SUNY)
summary: Physicist and Bayesian inference specialist whose 2019 Entropy paper is the most-cited peer-reviewed study calculating UAP flight physics from documented cases. Leads UAPx field expeditions and is lead author on the 2025 'New Science of UAP' collaborative paper. Affiliated with the Galileo Project and Sol Foundation.

name: Dr. Matt Szydagis
role: Assistant Professor of Physics, UAPx Field Research Leader
affiliation: University at Albany (SUNY)
summary: Experimental particle physicist whose work on LUX and LZ dark matter experiments and creator of widely-used NEST simulation software gives him exceptional detector instrumentation credentials. Pivoted to UAP research as first author of the first peer-reviewed academic instrumented UAP field expedition paper and co-lead of UAPx alongside Kevin Knuth.

name: Dr. David Spergel
role: Chair, NASA UAP Independent Study Team; President, Simons Foundation
affiliation: Simons Foundation / Princeton University
summary: MacArthur Fellow and Breakthrough Prize-winning astrophysicist who chaired NASA's UAP Independent Study Team (2022-2023). The panel's September 2023 report - finding no extraterrestrial evidence but identifying severe data quality gaps - represents the official NASA scientific posture on UAP and directly led to NASA appointing its first Director of UAP Research.

name: Prof. Hakan Kayal
role: Professor of Space Technology; Founder, IFEX; SONATE-2 Principal Investigator
affiliation: University of Wurzburg (JMU) / IFEX
summary: German space technology professor who founded IFEX - the first European academic institution to formally incorporate UAP into its research mandate - and launched SONATE-2 (March 2024), a CubeSat in orbit with onboard AI for anomalous object detection. His SkyCAM AI observatory is operational at Wurzburg, and his team participates in a DLR-funded Mars mission with a UAP detection camera.

name: Dr. Jeffrey Kripal
role: J. Newton Rayzor Chair, Religion and Philosophy, Rice University
affiliation: Rice University / Archives of the Impossible
summary: Rice University comparative religion scholar who founded the Archives of the Impossible - one of the world's largest academic repositories of UAP and contact research materials, housing the archives of John Mack, Whitley Strieber, and CIA remote viewing director Ed May. His books argue that extraordinary contact experiences demand new epistemological tools beyond both materialist dismissal and literal belief.

name: Robert Powell
role: Co-Founder, Scientific Coalition for UAP Studies (SCU)
affiliation: Scientific Coalition for UAP Studies (SCU)
summary: Semiconductor engineer (AMD, 4 nanotechnology patents) and UAP researcher who co-authored the 2019 Entropy paper with Kevin Knuth calculating UAP flight physics from documented cases. Co-founder of the Scientific Coalition for UAP Studies (SCU); his radar analyses of the 2008 Stephenville Lights and 2013 Aguadilla incident are landmark examples of rigorous multi-sensor UAP case investigation.

name: Dr. Alexander Wendt
role: Mershon Professor of International Security, Ohio State University
affiliation: Ohio State University
summary: The most influential International Relations scholar of the past 20 years (2017 TRIP survey), ranked by peers above Kissinger and Mearsheimer. Co-authored 'Sovereignty and the UFO' (Political Theory, 2008) - the first peer-reviewed political science article treating UAP as a governance problem - arguing the UFO taboo is structurally produced by anthropocentric sovereignty rather than evidence-based.

name: Gary McKinnon
role: Systems Administrator and UAP Evidence Claimant
affiliation: Independent
summary: Scottish IT administrator who accessed 97 US government networks between 2001 and 2002 searching for suppressed UFO evidence. Claims to have found a 'Non-Terrestrial Officers' spreadsheet and a high-resolution UFO photograph at NASA. A decade-long US extradition fight ended in 2012 when UK Home Secretary Theresa May blocked it on human-rights grounds; he was never charged in the UK.

name: Mathew Bevan
role: IT Technician and UAP Evidence Claimant
affiliation: Independent; Kuji Media Corporation
summary: Welsh IT technician who in 1996 - five years before Gary McKinnon - accessed Wright-Patterson AFB, Griffiss Air Force Base Research Laboratory, and NASA systems using a Commodore Amiga, searching for suppressed UFO crash evidence. Claims to have found documents describing a working anti-gravity prototype. The US called him 'the single biggest threat to world peace since Adolf Hitler'; charges were dropped in 1997 when the US declined to cooperate with UK prosecution.

name: Clifford Stone
role: Sergeant First Class, US Army (ret.) and UAP Crash Retrieval Claimant
affiliation: United States Army
summary: US Army Sergeant First Class who claimed covert assignment to UAP crash retrieval teams under Project Moon Dust and Operation Blue Fly. Led the FOIA campaign that forced the Air Force to officially admit those programs existed in 1992. Testified at the 2001 Disclosure Project press conference claiming the US government had catalogued 57 distinct non-human species. He lived in Roswell, New Mexico and published Eyes Only in 2012 before his death in February 2021.

name: John Ramirez
role: GS-15 Intelligence Officer, CIA (ret.)
affiliation: Central Intelligence Agency
summary: Former CIA GS-15 intelligence officer with a 25-year career across the Directorate of Science and Technology, Directorate of Intelligence, and ODNI National Counterproliferation Center. A specialist in ballistic missile defense and ELINT, he went public in October 2021 describing an institutional culture within the CIA that discourages analysts from engaging with UAP data, and asserting that the Air Force withholds UAP information because recovered technology has already been applied in classified programs.

name: Oscar Santa Maria Huerta
role: Colonel, Peruvian Air Force (ret.) - La Joya UAP Engagement Pilot
affiliation: Fuerza Aérea del Peru (Peruvian Air Force)
summary: Peruvian Air Force Colonel who on April 11, 1980 fired 64 rounds of 30mm cannon at a UAP over La Joya Air Force Base with no effect. The craft anticipated his weapons lock each time, ascending to 63,000 ft during pursuit. Witnessed by approximately 1,800 personnel on the ground. A US DoD cable confirmed the incident and described the object as of unknown origin. The only documented case of a military pilot firing on a UAP with confirmed hits and no effect.

name: Parviz Jafari
role: Brigadier General, Imperial Iranian Air Force (ret.) - 1976 Tehran UAP Intercept
affiliation: Imperial Iranian Air Force (IIAF)
summary: Iranian Air Force Brigadier General who on September 19, 1976, as a squadron commander in an F-4 Phantom II over Tehran, acquired radar lock on a UAP the size of a KC-135 tanker. When he attempted to fire an AIM-9 missile, all weapons control and communications failed. A DIA cable distributed to the White House, Secretary of State, Joint Chiefs, NSA, and CIA documented the incident and rated it an outstanding UAP report. He testified publicly at the National Press Club in 2007.

name: Mark McCandlish
role: Aerospace Illustrator and ARV Fluxliner Witness
affiliation: Independent; Lockheed, Rockwell (contractor)
summary: Aerospace illustrator for major defense contractors who in 1988 received a detailed account from colleague Brad Sorensen of three disc-shaped craft at a restricted Norton AFB hangar. McCandlish produced the iconic Fluxliner technical cutaway - one of the most reproduced UAP technology illustrations in the field. He testified at the 2001 Disclosure Project and was reportedly preparing Senate testimony when he died April 13, 2021; officially ruled a suicide, disputed by associates.

name: Bob Jacobs
role: First Lieutenant, US Air Force (ret.) - Big Sur UFO Film
affiliation: United States Air Force
summary: US Air Force First Lieutenant who in September 1964 oversaw telescopic filming of Atlas missile tests near Big Sur, California. The film captured a disc-shaped object circling the missile re-entry vehicle and firing four pulses at it - the test failed. His superior Major Florenz Mansmann independently corroborated the account in a 1987 letter. Jacobs has maintained an entirely consistent account for over 40 years; the film was confiscated and has never been publicly released.

name: Jonathan Weygandt
role: Lance Corporal, US Marine Corps (ret.) - Peru Crash Site Witness
affiliation: United States Marine Corps
summary: US Marine Corps Lance Corporal who in 1997, during Operation Laser Strike in Peru, was among the first responders to reach a crash site involving a large egg-shaped craft embedded in a cliff face. He describes the object as warm, self-repairing, and biologically textured, emitting purplish-green light. He was intercepted by unmarked personnel and a DoE MOPP team, then coercively interrogated. He testified at the 2001 Disclosure Project; James Fox called it the most outstanding testimony from that project. He did not speak publicly again until 2023.

name: Donna Hare
role: NASA Contractor (Philco Ford) - Photo Lab Whistleblower
affiliation: Philco Ford (NASA contractor); Johnson Space Center
summary: Former NASA contractor who worked in the photo laboratory at Johnson Space Center Building 8 during the 1970s with a Secret clearance. She testified at the 2001 Disclosure Project that she witnessed a photo technician manually airbrush a disc-shaped object from a lunar photograph before public distribution, and that NASA personnel suppressed astronaut UAP observations. Her Building 8 account overlaps independently with Gary McKinnon's claim to have seen a UAP photograph on a JSC network machine in the same building.

name: Dr. Salvatore Pais
role: Aerospace Engineer, NAWCAD / U.S. Navy
affiliation: Naval Air Warfare Center Aircraft Division (NAWCAD)
summary: Romanian-born Navy aerospace engineer who filed a cluster of patents assigned to the Department of the Navy describing a hybrid aerospace-underwater craft, room-temperature superconductor, gravitational wave generator, and plasma fusion device. The Navy's own Chief Technology Officer made sworn declarations to the USPTO vouching for the patents' operability; three years of internal testing costing 08,000 concluded the 'Pais Effect' could not be proven. Now works for the U.S. Space Force.

name: Dan Sherman
role: USAF ELINT Specialist / Alleged Intuitive Communicator
affiliation: U.S. Air Force / NSA
summary: 12-year USAF veteran and NSA-affiliated Electronic Intelligence specialist who claims he was recruited in 1992 at Fort Meade as an 'Intuitive Communicator' for Project Preserve Destiny - an alleged government program training genetically selected personnel to relay communications from extraterrestrial entities. His verifiable military background is credible; the IC claims are extraordinary and have no documentary corroboration. Renewed public interest following a 2025 Jesse Michels interview.

name: Charles Hall
role: USAF Weather Observer, Indian Springs Gunnery Ranges
affiliation: U.S. Air Force / Nellis AFB
summary: U.S. Air Force weather observer with an M.A. in Applied Nuclear Physics who claims sustained encounters with tall, chalk-white humanoid beings he calls the 'Tall Whites' at the isolated Nellis gunnery ranges near Indian Springs, Nevada from 1965 to 1967. His six-volume Millennial Hospitality series (2002-2020) presents these events as fictionalized memoir. Verifiable USAF service record at the stated location; no physical evidence or independent corroboration exists. Died November 2021.

name: Nick Cook
role: Aviation Editor, Jane's Defence Weekly
affiliation: Jane's Defence Weekly
summary: British aviation journalist and 14-year Aviation Editor at Jane's Defence Weekly who won four Royal Aeronautical Society awards. His 2002 book 'The Hunt for Zero Point' documented declassified 1950s electrogravitics research and the thesis that antigravity technology entered US black programs via Operation Paperclip, reaching number one on Amazon's non-fiction chart. Now serves as a director of the Bigelow Institute for Consciousness Studies.
